source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - West Cameron 61A Platform

event.top_event: Object strike injury from falling pump connection
actions_taken: First aid provided by onsite safety representative at 6:30am
Transported to Oschner St. Patrick's Hospital in Lake Charles, LA
Surgery performed to repair right humerus bone
Safe reenactment of job task conducted
event.summary: On 13-APR-2019 at 0615 hours, a hammer union pump connection (Type 1502, 40.6 lbs) fell 36.8 feet from a tubing joint being lifted by liftboat crane during workover operations on the A1 well at WC-61A platform. The pump connection struck a DSS worker on the hard hat, upper right arm, right thigh, and right foot, fracturing the humerus and lacerating the big toe. The root cause was improper securing of the pump connection to the tubing joint; during the second attempt to make the connection with the work string, the crane sling unscrewed the pump connection as workers turned the tubing joint clockwise. Contributing factors included poor communication regarding shift progress, inadequate supervision, missing work permits, complacency, inattention to task detail, and failure to follow JSA procedures.
recommendations: Issue safety alert on findings of how the pump connection fell 36.8 feet striking a worker
key_phrases: JSA not approved for actual work scope

hazards.name: Suspended load (tubing joint with pump connection)

threats.name: Pump connection not properly secured to tubing joint
Sling wrapping around pump connection during rotation
Unthreading of pump connection due to counter-clockwise rotation

consequences.name: Lost time accident requiring evacuation and hospitalization

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for work tasks
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards and work procedures
controls.lod_basis: Planning and hazard identification prior to work

supporting_text: The JSAs were approved by the day company representative and day supervisor without visiting the work site
The JSAs have instructions on the first page that states, 'To be reviewed in the area that the work is being conducted'
A JSA was not approved for connecting the pump connection to the tubing joint or tripping work string into the well
The three JSAs have tasks assigned to the IP outside his normal job functions

controls.name: Supervision and work area inspection
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to monitor work execution and identify deviations

supporting_text: The on-duty day supervisor never inspected the work area prior to or during the operation. The supervisor was in his office during the incident
Inadequate supervision: the on-duty day supervisor never inspected the work area prior to or during the operation

controls.name: Pre-tour safety meeting and shift handover communication
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure accurate information transfer between shifts
controls.lod_basis: Communication of work status and hazards

supporting_text: During the day shift safety/pre tour meeting, the workers were informed the tripping operations were completed and the pump connection was installed on the work string. This information was incorrect
Communication: the day/night supervisors and EnVen company representatives were unaware of the night shifts progress prior to completing the JSAs for the day shift

controls.name: Stop Work Authority
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to halt work when hazards or deviations are identified
controls.lod_basis: Worker empowerment to stop unsafe work

supporting_text: Stop Work Authority was not used by the workers due to completely different job scope/hazards and proceeded with tripping operations
The ARO workers did not use stop work authority when they found the pump connection not installed on the tubing work string and the tripping operations not completed

controls.name: Proper fastening and torque verification of hammer union connection
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to ensure mechanical integrity of connection

supporting_text: The pump connection was not properly secured to the tubing joint and the sling unscrewed the pump connection as the workers turned the tubing joint
It takes eight rounds to connect the tubing joint and work string together while taking three and a quarter turns to unscrew the pump connection from the sub
A properly fastened hammer union and a properly functioning hook on the fast line of the crane would not allow the sling to unscrew the hammer union pump connection

controls.name: Crane sling design and rigging configuration
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent sling interaction with suspended load
controls.lod_basis: Proper rigging to prevent unintended rotation of load components

supporting_text: An elevator was rigged up with a two part sling (sling) attached to the fast line of the liftboat crane

During the reenactment, as the worker turned the tubing joint the sling wrapped around the pump connection. When the sling wrapped around the pump connection, the hook started to rotate on the crane as it is designed to perform
As the tubing joint is turning clockwise; the sling turns the pump connection counter clockwise

controls.name: Hard hat and personal protective equipment
controls.barrier_role: PPE to reduce severity of impact from dropped object

supporting_text: A hammer union pump connection with a 90° angle (pump connection, i.e., Type 1502) weighing 40.6 pounds fell 36.8 feet striking the injured person's (IP) hard hat, upper right arm, right thigh, and right foot

controls.name: First aid and emergency medical response
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to provide immediate medical care and evacuation
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response to minimize injury severity

supporting_text: At 6:30am, the onsite safety representative provided the IP with first aid
At 9:44am, a helicopter arrived and transported the IP to Oschner St. Patrick's Hospital in Lake Charles
The IP had surgery to repair his right humerus bone; he also received treatment for bruising to the thigh and a laceration on big toe

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
